summary: CCRB's full-investigation rate has fallen for three straight years even as the agency closes more cases overall, because complaints filed against NYPD officers jumped roughly 60% between fiscal 2022 and 2025. With that surge outpacing staffing, a growing share of complaints closed as "unable to investigate" (up 43% in FY25 alone) due to budget constraints or unavailable witnesses, pulling down the percentage that get a full investigation.

sources.label: CCRB, Fiscal 2026 Preliminary Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: decreasing as complaints against officers surged 60% and outpaced staffing
